meta.title: 丝绸之路知识图谱
meta.description: 一个关于丝绸之路的结构化开放数据集，涵盖城市、人物、物产、事件四大类实体及其关联关系。
meta.description_en: An open structured dataset about the Silk Road, covering cities, figures, goods, and events and their interconnections.

cities.name: 长安
cities.description: 丝绸之路的东方起点，汉唐帝国的都城。从这里出发的商队穿越河西走廊，将丝绸、瓷器、漆器运往西域。
cities.description_en: The eastern terminus of the Silk Road and capital of the Han and Tang empires. Caravans departed from here, crossing the Hexi Corridor with silk, porcelain, and lacquerware bound for the Western Regions.

cities.name: 洛阳
cities.description: 东汉时期的丝路起点，也是佛教传入中国的重要节点。白马寺是中国第一座佛教寺院。
cities.description_en: The Silk Road's starting point during the Eastern Han dynasty, and a key node for Buddhism's entry into China. The White Horse Temple is China's first Buddhist temple.

cities.name: 敦煌
cities.description: 丝路上的璀璨明珠，莫高窟所在地。出敦煌便进入西域，是东西方文明交汇的十字路口。
cities.description_en: A brilliant jewel on the Silk Road and home to the Mogao Caves. Beyond Dunhuang lay the Western Regions — it was the crossroads of Eastern and Western civilizations.

cities.name: 喀什
cities.description: 塔里木盆地西缘的贸易枢纽，天山南路和天山北路的交汇点。至今保留着浓郁的西域风情。
cities.description_en: A trading hub on the western edge of the Tarim Basin where the northern and southern routes around the Taklamakan Desert converged. It retains a strong Central Asian character to this day.

cities.name: 撒马尔罕
cities.description: 中亚最古老的城市之一，粟特人的商业中心。帖木儿帝国时期达到鼎盛，雷吉斯坦广场见证了丝路最后的辉煌。
cities.description_en: One of Central Asia's oldest cities and a commercial center of the Sogdians. It reached its zenith under the Timurid Empire — the Registan Square bears witness to the Silk Road's last golden age.

cities.name: 巴格达
cities.description: 阿拔斯王朝首都，智慧宫所在地。中国的造纸术通过丝路传到这里，再由阿拉伯人传往欧洲。
cities.description_en: Capital of the Abbasid Caliphate and home to the House of Wisdom. Chinese papermaking technology reached Baghdad via the Silk Road and was later transmitted to Europe by the Arabs.

cities.name: 君士坦丁堡
cities.description: 丝路西端的终点，连接亚洲与欧洲的黄金之城。中国的丝绸由此进入欧洲贵族的生活。
cities.description_en: The western terminus of the Silk Road — a golden city bridging Asia and Europe. Chinese silk entered the lives of European nobility through this gateway.

cities.name: 吐鲁番
cities.description: 火焰山下的绿洲城市，高昌故城所在地。是佛教从印度经西域传入中原的中转站。
cities.description_en: An oasis city beneath the Flaming Mountains, site of the ancient city of Gaochang. A relay station for Buddhism's transmission from India through the Western Regions into China.

figures.name: 张骞
figures.description: 汉武帝派遣出使西域的使节，两次西行，凿空西域，被誉为'丝绸之路的开辟者'。带回了葡萄、苜蓿、胡麻等西域物产的信息。
figures.description_en: An envoy sent by Emperor Wu of Han to the Western Regions. His two expeditions 'opened up' the Western Regions, earning him the title 'Pioneer of the Silk Road.' He brought back knowledge of grapes, alfalfa, and sesame.

figures.name: 玄奘
figures.description: 唐代高僧，沿丝路西行至印度取经，历时17年，行程五万里。归国后翻译佛经75部，著有《大唐西域记》，是研究丝路历史的珍贵文献。
figures.description_en: A Tang dynasty monk who traveled west along the Silk Road to India in search of Buddhist scriptures. His 17-year, 50,000-li journey resulted in the translation of 75 Buddhist texts and the 'Great Tang Records on the Western Regions.'

figures.name: 马可·波罗
figures.description: 威尼斯商人，沿丝路到达元大都（北京），仕元17年。《马可·波罗游记》激发了欧洲对东方的向往，间接推动了地理大发现。
figures.description_en: A Venetian merchant who reached the Yuan capital of Dadu (Beijing) via the Silk Road and served the Mongol court for 17 years. His 'Travels' ignited European fascination with the East and indirectly spurred the Age of Discovery.

figures.name: 甘英
figures.description: 班超派遣的使节，出使大秦（罗马帝国），抵达条支（今叙利亚一带）的海边。虽因安息商人的劝阻未能渡海，但他是史载最接近罗马的汉朝使者。
figures.description_en: An envoy sent by Ban Chao to the Roman Empire (Daqin). He reached the coast of Tiaozhi (modern Syria) but was dissuaded from crossing the sea by Parthian merchants. He was the Han envoy who came closest to Rome.

goods.name: 丝绸
goods.description: 丝路最核心的商品，也是这条路名称的由来。中国的养蚕缫丝技术长期被严格保密，丝绸在罗马帝国与黄金等价。
goods.description_en: The signature commodity that gave the Silk Road its name. China's sericulture techniques were a closely guarded secret for centuries, and silk was worth its weight in gold in the Roman Empire.

goods.name: 瓷器
goods.description: 中国瓷器通过海陆丝路远销世界，'China'一词即源于瓷器。伊斯兰世界和欧洲竞相仿制，但直到18世纪才破解制瓷秘密。
goods.description_en: Chinese porcelain was exported worldwide via both land and maritime Silk Roads — the very word 'china' derives from it. The Islamic world and Europe competed to imitate it but the secret was not cracked until the 18th century.

goods.name: 造纸术
goods.description: 公元751年怛罗斯之战后，被俘的中国造纸工匠将技术传入撒马尔罕，随后经巴格达传到欧洲。造纸术的传播深刻改变了世界文明进程。
goods.description_en: After the Battle of Talas (751 CE), captured Chinese papermakers brought the technology to Samarkand. From there it spread to Baghdad and eventually Europe, profoundly changing the course of world civilization.

goods.name: 玻璃器皿
goods.description: 罗马和波斯的玻璃器皿沿丝路传入中国，深受贵族喜爱。在中国多地汉墓中出土了罗马玻璃制品。
goods.description_en: Roman and Persian glassware traveled east along the Silk Road and was highly prized by Chinese nobility. Roman glass artifacts have been unearthed from Han dynasty tombs across China.

goods.name: 葡萄
goods.description: 张骞出使西域后将葡萄引入中原。葡萄和葡萄酒文化从此在中国扎根，唐诗中'葡萄美酒夜光杯'成为千古名句。
goods.description_en: Grapes were introduced to China after Zhang Qian's missions to the Western Regions. Grape and wine culture took root — Tang poetry immortalized 'grape wine in luminous cups' as an enduring image.

goods.name: 香料
goods.description: 胡椒、乳香、没药等香料通过海陆丝路进入中国，深刻影响了中国的饮食、医药和宗教仪式。
goods.description_en: Pepper, frankincense, myrrh, and other spices entered China via both land and maritime routes, profoundly influencing Chinese cuisine, medicine, and religious rituals.

events.name: 张骞出使西域
events.description: 汉武帝派遣张骞出使大月氏，欲联合抗击匈奴。张骞被匈奴扣押十年，逃脱后继续西行，最远抵达大夏（今阿富汗北部）。虽未达成军事同盟，但开启了汉朝与西域各国的交往。
events.description_en: Emperor Wu of Han dispatched Zhang Qian to seek an alliance with the Yuezhi against the Xiongnu. Held captive for ten years, Zhang Qian escaped and continued west, reaching as far as Bactria. Though no military alliance was formed, this opened relations with the Western Regions.

events.name: 怛罗斯之战
events.description: 唐军与阿拉伯帝国（大食）军队在怛罗斯河畔交战。唐军战败，但被俘的造纸工匠将造纸术传入中亚，成为技术传播史上的标志性事件。
events.description_en: Tang and Abbasid (Arab) forces clashed on the banks of the Talas River. The Tang forces were defeated, but captured Chinese papermakers brought papermaking technology to Central Asia — a landmark event in the history of technology transfer.

events.name: 玄奘西行
events.description: 玄奘法师从长安出发，偷渡出境，经敦煌、高昌、中亚，抵达印度那烂陀寺。17年后携带657部梵文佛经回到长安，唐太宗亲自接见。
events.description_en: Monk Xuanzang departed Chang'an, traveling via Dunhuang, Gaochang, and Central Asia to reach Nalanda Monastery in India. He returned 17 years later with 657 Sanskrit Buddhist texts and was personally received by Emperor Taizong.

events.name: 蒙古和平时期
events.description: 蒙古帝国统一欧亚大陆大部分地区后，丝路进入最安全和繁荣的时期。商队、使节、传教士（如马可·波罗）得以自由往来，东西方交流空前活跃。
events.description_en: After the Mongol Empire unified much of Eurasia, the Silk Road entered its safest and most prosperous period. Merchants, envoys, and missionaries (like Marco Polo) could travel freely — East-West exchange flourished as never before.

events.name: 郑和下西洋
events.description: 明代航海家郑和七次远航，到达东南亚、印度、阿拉伯和东非。这标志着海上丝绸之路的巅峰，也展现了中国与世界交流的另一条大动脉。
events.description_en: The Ming admiral Zheng He led seven grand voyages reaching Southeast Asia, India, Arabia, and East Africa. This marked the zenith of the Maritime Silk Road, showcasing another artery of China's exchange with the world.
